name: Waikiki
narration: You are entering Waikiki, the dense beachfront district that turned Honolulu into a global visitor hub.

name: Duke Kahanamoku Statue
narration: The Duke Kahanamoku statue honors the Olympic swimmer and surfer who helped introduce modern surfing to the world.

name: Waikiki Beach
narration: Waikiki Beach is a short, famous sweep of sand backed by hotels, surf breaks, and Diamond Head views.

name: Moana Surfrider
narration: The Moana Surfrider opened in 1901 and is often called the First Lady of Waikiki.

name: Royal Hawaiian Hotel
narration: The pink Royal Hawaiian Hotel opened in 1927 and became one of Waikiki's most recognizable landmarks.

name: International Market Place
narration: International Market Place sits around a large banyan tree and marks a long-running retail crossroads in Waikiki.

name: Honolulu Zoo
narration: Honolulu Zoo anchors the Diamond Head end of Waikiki near Kapiolani Park.

name: Kapiolani Park
narration: Kapiolani Park is Honolulu's oldest public park and a green buffer between Waikiki and Diamond Head.

name: Waikiki Aquarium
narration: Waikiki Aquarium has focused on Pacific marine life beside the reef since the early twentieth century.

name: Diamond Head
narration: Diamond Head is the volcanic tuff cone framing Waikiki's eastern skyline.

name: Ala Wai Canal
narration: The Ala Wai Canal separates Waikiki from inland Honolulu and reshaped this former wetland district.

name: Fort DeRussy
narration: Fort DeRussy is a military reservation and beach park that preserves a rare open stretch inside Waikiki.

name: U.S. Army Museum of Hawaii
narration: The U.S. Army Museum of Hawaii occupies Battery Randolph at Fort DeRussy.

name: Hawaii Convention Center
narration: The Hawaii Convention Center uses open-air architecture to catch trade winds near the edge of Waikiki.

name: Ala Moana
narration: Ala Moana means path to the sea, and this district links shopping, beach park, and harbor edges.

name: Ala Moana Center
narration: Ala Moana Center is one of the world's largest open-air shopping centers.

name: Ala Moana Beach Park
narration: Ala Moana Beach Park is a local favorite with calm water protected by an outer reef.

name: Magic Island
narration: Magic Island is a man-made peninsula with wide views back toward Waikiki and Diamond Head.

name: Ala Wai Small Boat Harbor
narration: Ala Wai Small Boat Harbor sits between Waikiki and Ala Moana with one of Honolulu's busiest marina edges.
sourceName: Hawaii Division of Boating and Ocean Recreation

name: Kakaako
narration: You are entering Kakaako, a former industrial district now known for murals, towers, restaurants, and waterfront parks.

name: Ward Village
narration: Ward Village is a large mixed-use redevelopment reshaping the center of Kakaako.

name: SALT at Our Kakaako
narration: SALT at Our Kakaako clusters local food, coffee, shops, and public art in converted warehouse blocks.

name: Kakaako Waterfront Park
narration: Kakaako Waterfront Park has grassy hills and broad harbor views where industrial Honolulu meets the ocean.

name: Kewalo Basin Harbor
narration: Kewalo Basin Harbor is a working harbor for tour boats and fishing vessels at the edge of Kakaako.

name: Mother Waldron Neighborhood Park
narration: Mother Waldron Park is a neighborhood green space tucked inside Kakaako's fast-changing street grid.

name: Hawaii Children's Discovery Center
narration: The Hawaii Children's Discovery Center sits near the waterfront with hands-on exhibits for families.

name: Honolulu Museum of Art
narration: Honolulu Museum of Art connects Asian, Pacific, European, and American collections in a compact campus.

name: Thomas Square
narration: Thomas Square commemorates the 1843 restoration of the Hawaiian Kingdom's sovereignty.

name: Neal S. Blaisdell Center
narration: The Blaisdell Center is Honolulu's major arena, concert hall, and exhibition complex.

name: Capitol District
narration: You are entering Honolulu's civic core, where the palace, capitol, courts, and city hall sit within a few blocks.

name: Iolani Palace
narration: Iolani Palace was the royal residence of the Hawaiian Kingdom and is the only official royal palace in the United States.

name: King Kamehameha Statue
narration: The King Kamehameha statue stands in front of Aliiolani Hale facing Iolani Palace.

name: Aliiolani Hale
narration: Aliiolani Hale houses the Hawaii State Supreme Court and anchors one side of the palace grounds.

name: Hawaii State Capitol
narration: The Hawaii State Capitol uses reflecting pools and open forms to reference the islands, ocean, and volcanoes.

name: Kawaiahao Church
narration: Kawaiahao Church was built from coral blocks and is closely tied to nineteenth-century Honolulu.

name: Honolulu Hale
narration: Honolulu Hale is the city hall for the City and County of Honolulu.

name: Hawaiian Mission Houses
narration: The Hawaiian Mission Houses preserve some of Honolulu's oldest Western-style buildings.

name: Washington Place
narration: Washington Place was Queen Liliuokalani's home and later served as Hawaii's governor's residence.

name: Hawaii State Library
narration: The Hawaii State Library faces the capitol district from a historic Beaux-Arts building.

name: National Memorial Cemetery of the Pacific
narration: Punchbowl crater holds the National Memorial Cemetery of the Pacific and a sweeping view over Honolulu.

name: Aloha Tower
narration: Aloha Tower opened in 1926 as a harbor landmark greeting passengers arriving by ship.

name: Honolulu Harbor
narration: Honolulu Harbor is the state's main commercial harbor and has shaped the city since the monarchy era.

name: Chinatown
narration: You are entering Chinatown, one of Honolulu's oldest business districts and a dense food and arts neighborhood.

name: Hawaii Theatre
narration: Hawaii Theatre opened in 1922 and remains a major restored performing arts venue downtown.

name: Foster Botanical Garden
narration: Foster Botanical Garden preserves tropical trees and plants in the middle of urban Honolulu.

name: Liliuokalani Botanical Garden
narration: Liliuokalani Botanical Garden focuses on native Hawaiian plants and a stream-fed urban landscape.

name: Murphy's Bar and Grill
narration: Murphy's occupies a historic downtown corner and is a familiar Chinatown-adjacent lunch and pau hana stop.

name: The Pig and the Lady
narration: The Pig and the Lady helped make Chinatown a modern Honolulu dining destination.

name: Maguro Brothers Chinatown
narration: Maguro Brothers is known for sashimi and poke from a small Chinatown market counter.

name: Leonard's Bakery
narration: Leonard's Bakery is famous for malasadas, the Portuguese-style doughnuts that became a Honolulu staple.

name: Rainbow Drive-In
narration: Rainbow Drive-In is a plate-lunch institution near the Diamond Head side of Waikiki.

name: Marugame Udon Waikiki
narration: Marugame Udon's Waikiki shop is known for fast cafeteria-style noodles and frequent lines.

name: Liliha Bakery
narration: Liliha Bakery is known for coco puffs and local diner comfort food.

name: Highway Inn Kakaako
narration: Highway Inn's Kakaako location serves Hawaiian food in the middle of the neighborhood's restaurant cluster.

name: Side Street Inn
narration: Side Street Inn is a local comfort-food spot known for share plates and after-work gatherings.

name: Bishop Museum
narration: Bishop Museum is Hawaii's major museum for natural history and Hawaiian and Pacific culture.

name: Queen Emma Summer Palace
narration: Queen Emma Summer Palace preserves a mountain-side royal retreat above Honolulu.

name: Tantalus Lookout
narration: Tantalus Lookout gives a high, sweeping view over Diamond Head, Waikiki, downtown, and the harbor.

name: Manoa Falls Trail
narration: Manoa Falls Trail leads through a rainforest valley to one of Honolulu's most visited waterfalls.

name: Shangri La Museum of Islamic Art
narration: Shangri La is Doris Duke's oceanfront home and a museum of Islamic art near Diamond Head.

name: Sans Souci Beach
narration: Sans Souci Beach is a calmer swimming and snorkeling pocket near the Waikiki Aquarium.

name: Royal Mausoleum at Mauna Ala
narration: Mauna Ala is the Royal Mausoleum, resting place for many members of Hawaiian royal families.

name: Japanese Cultural Center of Hawaii
narration: The Japanese Cultural Center of Hawaii preserves stories of Japanese immigration and community life in the islands.

name: University of Hawaii at Manoa
narration: The University of Hawaii at Manoa sits in the valley behind Waikiki and is the flagship public university campus.

name: Chaminade University
narration: Chaminade University occupies a hillside campus above Kaimuki with broad views toward Honolulu.
